job_title: Business Intelligence Analyst for the Division of Data Management and Technology (DMT)
company: NYC Department of Housing Preservation & Development
strengths: Strong Power BI and dashboard experience
Advanced SQL and database query optimization
Proven automation and ETL pipeline development
Quantified impact on efficiency and revenue
weaknesses: No Microsoft Access or SQL Developer listed
improvement_bullets: Optimized relational database queries (CTEs, window functions, joins) to clean and reconcile testing data, improving data accuracy by 60% and ensuring database integrity and performance.
Developed Power BI and Looker workflows to replace manual written reporting, reducing data mismatches and improving work efficiency by 40%, while gaining mastery of BI platforms.

Built Python automation for weekly sales and operations reports (ingesting POS and CRM data), saving 8 hours per week and improving reporting accuracy, demonstrating proficiency in data pipelines and ETL processes.

Standardized organizational KPIs and data governance frameworks to ensure cross-functional alignment, resulting in a consistent "single source of truth" for all business stakeholders and a reduction in report discrepancies, showcasing an understanding of data governance principles.

Modeled raw API data into a dbt star schema to optimize query performance, greatly lowering load times through BigQuery partitioning and clustering, and automated data quality validation with Great Expectations, aligning with the need for database administration and data quality practices.
cover_letter:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I am excited to submit my application for the Business Intelligence Analyst for the Division of Data Management and Technology (DMT) opportunity with the NYC Department of Housing Preservation & Development. I am interested in this opportunity because of the agency's vital mission to promote quality and affordability in the city's housing, ensuring safe and healthy living conditions for all New Yorkers. What interests me about working for the NYC Department of Housing Preservation & Development is its commitment to fostering neighborhood strength and diversity. This dedication to improving the lives of New Yorkers through data-driven strategies deeply resonates with my own passion for using analytics to drive positive social impact.

I believe my strong Power BI and advanced Excel skills, coupled with my proven experience in developing data pipelines and automation, would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a data-driven mindset, focusing on transforming raw data into actionable insights. In fact, at Shine Masters Cleaning Services, I developed Power BI and Looker workflows to replace manual reporting, reducing data mismatches and improving work efficiency by 40%. This initiative provided critical insights that directly informed operational adjustments and strategic decisions.

Ultimately, I know that the Business Intelligence Analyst for the Division of Data Management and Technology (DMT) role at the NYC Department of Housing Preservation & Development would allow me to bring my expertise in data visualization and reporting to develop and maintain dashboards that deliver critical insights into ENS operations and outcomes. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead at Shine Masters Cleaning Services enabled me to develop my stakeholder communication skills. I regularly translated complex technical findings into clear business recommendations during stakeholder meetings, ensuring alignment between data insights and growth strategies. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my advanced SQL skills in working on analyzing data pertinent to ENS programs at the NYC Department of Housing Preservation & Development.

I know that my proficiency in Power BI, advanced Excel, and SQL, alongside my strong analytical and communication skills, align with what it takes to succeed in the Business Intelligence Analyst for the Division of Data Management and Technology (DMT) role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail.

Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Warm regards,
Justin Miller
